complete_examples.overview: Complete end-to-end examples of synthetic-human-tester invocations. Each example shows the spawn prompt received from the user and the expected completion report.

example.name: web_smoke_test_all_pass
example.scenario: The user has built a user registration feature. The dev server is running at localhost:3000. The user spawns synthetic-human-tester to verify the signup flow.
example.spawn_prompt:
environments: web: base_url: http://localhost:3000
test_plan: - environment: web given: I am on the home page when: I click the "Sign Up" link then: I should see a heading "Create Your Account"
- environment: web given: I am on the signup page when: I type "test@example.com" into the "Email" field and type "password123" into the "Password" field and click the "Create Account" button then: I should see the text "Check your email for a confirmation link"

execution.step: Capability detection: found browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_snapshot, browser_type → web testing available via Playwright MCP.
Step 1: browser_navigate to http://localhost:3000 → snapshot shows link "Sign Up" → browser_click on link "Sign Up" → snapshot shows heading "Create Your Account" → PASS.

Step 2: Already on signup page → browser_type into "Email" field → browser_type into "Password" field → browser_click on button "Create Account" → snapshot shows text "Check your email for a confirmation link" → PASS.

example.completion_report:
STATUS: all_passed
VISUAL: 🟢 No obvious issues
WEB: 2/2 passed ✅ Step 1: "Sign Up link navigates to signup page" — PASS Step 2: "Signup form submission shows confirmation" — PASS
SUMMARY: 2 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped

example.name: mcp_unavailable
example.scenario: The user requests web smoke tests, but Playwright MCP is not configured. Synthetic-human-tester detects this and reports mcp_unavailable.
example.spawn_prompt:
environments: web: base_url: http://localhost:3000 auth: method: login_form login_url: /login credentials: email: admin@test.com password: admin123
test_plan: - environment: web given: I am logged in when: I navigate to /dashboard then: I should see a heading "Dashboard"

execution.step: Capability detection: scanning for browser_* tools... none found.
Web environment cannot be tested. No other environments configured.

example.completion_report:
STATUS: mcp_unavailable
WEB: ⏭️ 0/1 skipped Reason: No browser automation MCP available. Install a browser automation MCP (e.g., Playwright MCP) and add it to your MCP configuration.
SUMMARY: 0 passed, 0 failed, 1 skipped

example.name: test_failure_with_evidence
example.scenario: A dashboard feature is supposedly working. Synthetic-human-tester tests it and finds that the stats widget is missing.
example.spawn_prompt:
environments: web: base_url: http://localhost:3000 auth: method: login_form login_url: /login credentials: email: admin@test.com password: admin123
test_plan: - environment: web given: I am logged in when: I navigate to /dashboard then: I should see a heading "Dashboard"
- environment: web when: I look at the stats section then: I should see a "Total Users" stat card

execution.step: Login: browser_navigate /login → type credentials → click "Login" → snapshot confirms logged in.
Step 1: browser_navigate /dashboard → snapshot shows heading "Dashboard" → PASS.

Step 2: Looking at snapshot... found section "Stats", but no element with accessible name "Total Users". Full snapshot of stats area shows: - heading "Stats" - text "Loading..." - No stat cards visible → FAIL: Expected "Total Users" stat card, but stats section shows "Loading..." with no cards rendered.

example.completion_report:
STATUS: tests_failed
WEB: 1/2 passed ❌ Step 1: "Dashboard heading visible" — PASS Step 2: "Total Users stat card present" — FAIL Expected: stat card with accessible name "Total Users" Actual: stats section shows "Loading..." text, no stat cards rendered Snapshot excerpt: region "Stats" heading "Stats" [level=2] text "Loading..."
SUMMARY: 1 passed, 1 failed, 0 skipped

example.name: desktop_smoke_test_all_pass
example.scenario: A Windows desktop app has been updated. The user configures a desktop environment with Windows-MCP available and wants to verify the save functionality.
example.spawn_prompt:
environments: desktop: executable_path: "C:/Program Files/MyApp/app.exe" window_title: "My Application"
test_plan: - environment: desktop given: I have opened the application when: I click the "File" menu and click "New" then: I should see a blank document window
- environment: desktop when: I type "Hello World" into the document and click the "Save" button then: I should see the text "Document saved successfully"

execution.step: Capability detection: found Screenshot, Snapshot, Click, Type → desktop testing available via Windows-MCP.
Visual: Screenshot captured → VISUAL: 🟢 No obvious issues.
Launch app: app.exe started, window "My Application" appeared.
Step 1: Snapshot → Click on "File" menu → Click on "New" → Snapshot shows blank document area → PASS.
Step 2: Type "Hello World" → Click "Save" button → Snapshot shows text "Document saved successfully" → PASS.

example.completion_report:
STATUS: all_passed
VISUAL: 🟢 No obvious issues
DESKTOP: 2/2 passed ✅ Step 1: "New document creation" — PASS Step 2: "Save document confirmation" — PASS
SUMMARY: 2 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped

example.name: web_tests_pass_but_ui_visually_broken
example.scenario: A dashboard feature functionally works — all buttons are clickable, all text is in the accessibility tree — but the UI is visually broken: the sidebar overlaps the main content and the footer text is cut off. Synthetic-human-tester captures this in the VISUAL section while still reporting functional all_passed.
example.spawn_prompt:
environments: web: base_url: http://localhost:3000 auth: method: login_form login_url: /login credentials: email: admin@test.com password: admin123
test_plan: - environment: web given: I am logged in when: I navigate to /dashboard then: I should see a heading "Dashboard"
- environment: web when: I click the "Reports" link in the sidebar then: I should see a heading "Reports"

execution.step: Capability detection: browser_* tools found → web testing available.
Login: browser_navigate /login → type credentials → click "Login" → snapshot confirms logged in.

Visual: browser_take_screenshot captured. Visual analysis: - Sidebar is rendered OVER the main content area, obscuring the left portion of the dashboard - Footer text "© 2026" is cut off at the bottom of the viewport → VISUAL: 🔴 Obvious breakage detected.
Step 1: browser_navigate /dashboard → snapshot shows heading "Dashboard" → PASS.
Step 2: browser_click on link "Reports" → snapshot shows heading "Reports" → PASS.

example.completion_report:
STATUS: all_passed
VISUAL: 🔴 Obvious breakage detected - Sidebar overlaps main content area (left ~200px of dashboard is obscured) - Footer text cut off at bottom of viewport
WEB: 2/2 passed ✅ Step 1: "Dashboard heading visible" — PASS Step 2: "Reports navigation" — PASS
SUMMARY: 2 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped
example.note: Even though STATUS is all_passed, the VISUAL section flags obvious UI breakage. The caller can detect the 🔴 VISUAL status from the completion summary and auto-launch a UI Designer subtask without requiring human intervention.
